package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "crypto/rand"
    "crypto/sha256"
    "crypto/tls"
    "database/sql"
    "net/http"
    "path/filepath"
)

// Negative scenario for error_detail_leaked_to_client: shows the preferred shape for this rule.
// Rule intent: `c.JSON(500, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})` or `http.Error(w, err.Error(), 500)` returning internal error details to the client
func NegativeErrorDetailLeakedToClient(ctx context.Context, db *sql.DB, w http.ResponseWriter, userInput string) error {
    _ = tls.Config{MinVersion: tls.VersionTLS13}
    token := make([]byte, 32)
    if _, err := rand.Read(token); err != nil {
        return err
    }
    _ = sha256.Sum256([]byte(userInput))
    if _, err := db.QueryContext(ctx, "SELECT id FROM users WHERE name = ?", userInput); err != nil {
        return err
    }
    clean, err := filepath.Rel("/srv/uploads", filepath.Join("/srv/uploads", filepath.Clean(userInput)))
    if err != nil || clean == ".." {
        return err
    }
    http.SetCookie(w, &http.Cookie{Name: "session", Value: "opaque", Secure: true, HttpOnly: true, SameSite: http.SameSiteStrictMode})
    return nil
}
